ud3.1:1.2: at one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s Park.
ud3.1:1.3: Now at that time a certain *bhikkhu* was sitting not far from the *Bhagavā*, having folded his legs crosswise, set his body upright, enduring *dukkha* born from the result of old *kamma* [*purāṇakammavipākajaṁ*]—intense, harsh, sharp *vedanā*—with *sati* [*sato*], with full awareness [*sampajāno*], not distressed.
ud3.1:2.1: The *Bhagavā* saw that *bhikkhu* sitting not far away, having folded his legs crosswise, set his body upright, enduring *dukkha* born from the result of old *kamma* [*purāṇakammavipākajaṁ*]—intense, harsh, sharp *vedanā*—with *sati* [*sataṁ*], with full awareness [*sampajānaṁ*], not distressed.
ud3.1:3.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having understood this matter, on that occasion uttered this inspired utterance:
ud3.1:4.2: shaking off the dust done before;
ud3.1:4.3: who is without possessiveness, steady, suchlike,
ud3.1:4.4: there is no need to speak to people.”
